data:
The demo is reset every hour to a clean state. Please do not abuse or use any sensitive or illegal data. You can try Biblioteca right now without installing it.
You can login with the following accounts:
Admin: Username: admin User: Username: user The password is in both cases the same value as the username.
Try it now ↗

A few users have been using Biblioteca for a while now without any major issue. However, as with any tool, there might be bugs or issues. If you find any, please report them and always make backups of your data and files. Books can be of multiple formats, but for now every book only has one format. Supported formats are
epub pdf mobi cbr cbz
data.add-books-from-command#Add books from co:
Any book in the public/books folder will be added to the database when you run the books:scan command. If a book already exists, its location in the filesystem will be updated, but metadata will not be overwritten.
You can periodically run this command to automatically add new books.
data.adding-books-from-the-interface#Adding b: Adding books to the public/books/consume will list them in the /books/new/consume/files page. This is useful when you want to add books to the library but don’t want to add them to the database yet or to mount a folder where books might arrive later (Maybe like a download folder 🫢).
data.verified-status#Verified status:
When books are added to the database, their “verified” status is set to false and a link to all unverified books is available in the menu.
More about verified status

data.adding-new-users#Adding new users:
If you are logged in with an administrator account, you will have a link to the user management page /user.
Here you can add new users, change their roles, and set or change their passwords.
You can select the maximum age category of books that the user can read. This is useful if you want to restrict access to children
The book will be available if their category is not set.
data.first-user#First user: You should have created your first admin user after installing the app. The first user is always an admin and you will need admin rights to add other users
data.roles#Roles:
User can have one of the following roles:
ROLE_USER: The default role for new users. They can read books, change their own settings, create their own shelves and sync their Kobo devices. ROLE_ADMIN: Can do everything a user can do and manage users and books.

data: There are 2 possible ways of configuring AI to help you tag and summarise books.
data.add-context#Add context:
There are currently 3 ways to add context for better results. They be enabled at the same time.
Wikipedia context: requires to fill in a wikipedia personal API token in the configuration, will search for information on the book for wikipedia EPUB context: if the book is an epub, it will be entirely added to the context for summarization. Amazon context: the app will try to find and scrape the Amazon product page of this Books
data.configure-your-prompts#Configure your pr:
Share your best prompts! When your favourite LLM is configured, fill in the prompts that will be used to generate the summaries and tags.
The application will replace the {book} placeholder with the book’s title, author and series. Also the {language} placeholder with the book’s language or user’s language.
data.example-workflow#Example workflow:
I use 2 different LLMs in my workflow
I have a local Ollama instance with the qwen2.5 model I use perplexity.ai API with the large model. How I proceed:
Search queries are built by Qwen Summaries are built by perplexity.ai, as it is the model that has the most chances to know about books out of the box. Tags are built by qwen after the summary as been written, because the summary is part of the context.
data.for-search#For search: The search model can be used to convert user searches in natural language to typesense filters.
data.for-summaries#For summaries: Can you make a factual summary of the book {book} in around 150 words in language {language}.
data.for-tags#For tags:
For tags, the following will always be appended to your prompt:
The output must be only valid JSON format. It must be an object with one key named "genres" containing an array of genres and tags for this book in strings. Do not add anything else than json. Do not add any other text or comment. Here are some example prompts:
Can you give 5 classifying keywords about the book {book} in a list without explanation in language {language} Give me a list of genres or tags for {book}. The first one must be about target audience (example: for teenagers), the second one must be about the location of the main story (example: Switzerland), the next one are topical and must help the user find similar books.
data.library-organization-commands#Library Or:
Biblioteca includes AI-powered commands to organize your entire library:
books:ai-organize - Master command that runs all harmonization in order books:authors-harmonize - Unify author name variations books:series-harmonize - Detect series and clean up titles books:tags-harmonize - Assign standardized genres These commands use a predefined list of 37 literary genres (available in French, English, German, Spanish) to ensure consistent categorization across your library.
# Organize everything with French genres docker compose exec biblioteca bin/console books:ai-organize --language fr --apply See the Commands documentation for detailed usage.
data.models#Models:
Go to /ai/model to configure AI models. You can configure as many models as you want. Currently the app allows to have Ollama compatible and OpenAI compatible models.
Please refer to OpenAI’s documentation to get your API key and model for ChatGPT. Example: use https://api.openai.com/v1/ as URL and gpt-4o-mini as model. Please refer to perplexity.ai documentation to get your API key and models for Perplexity You can test your model and the results directly from the UI.
You can enable or disable the addition of context per model. You can also configure system prompts for each model.
Below the model list, you can configure which model will be used for different actions.
data.run-it-for-one-book#Run it for one book: On a book page, you can click on the “generate summary” or “generate tags” buttons to show propositions and accept them or not.
data.run-it-through-your-whole-library#Run it:
You can run the following command:
docker compose exec biblioteca bin/console books:ai It will tag all your books that currently don’t have tags with the default prompts.
If you want to use a user’s configured prompts:
docker compose exec biblioteca bin/console books:ai If you want to use it on a specific book:
docker compose exec biblioteca bin/console books:ai -b
data.search#Search:
Enter a natural search query, for example: Mangas by eiichiro oda and click on the magic wand. This will convert your query in filters like authors:=Eiichiro Oda && extension:=[cbr,cbz,pdf]
You can try with things like Thrillers in italy or cook vegetarian meals and it will try to find the best query

data:
Multiple helper commands are available to help you manage your library. For simplicity, only the command is listed here, you will need to prefix it depending on your installation:
With a standard docker compose environment: docker-compose exec php bin/console With ddev: ddev exec bin/console
data.appbackup-db#\u003ccode\u003eapp:backup-: Creates a sql backup of the database in the backups folder.
data.appcreate-admin-user#\u003ccode\u003eapp: Create an admin user. This commande takes the username and the password as parameters. You should need this command only during the first installation, as you can create users from the interface.
data.biblioversetypesensepopulate#\u003ccode\: Will re-create the search engine index and re-import all books from the database to the search engine.
data.booksai#\u003ccode\u003ebooks:ai\u003c/c: Check the documentation for this in the AI chapter.
data.booksai-organize#\u003ccode\u003ebooks:a:
AI-powered library organization - Runs all harmonization commands in optimal order: authors → series → tags.
# Preview changes (no modifications) books:ai-organize --language fr # Apply all changes books:ai-organize --language fr --apply # Skip specific steps books:ai-organize --language fr --skip-authors --apply books:ai-organize --language fr --skip-series --skip-tags --apply Options:
--language / -l: Target language for genres (fr, en, de, es). Default: en --apply / -a: Apply changes (preview mode by default) --skip-authors: Skip author harmonization --skip-series: Skip series/title harmonization --skip-tags: Skip tag harmonization For finer control (e.g., excluding specific books or authors), run the individual commands below.
data.booksauthors-harmonize#\u003ccode\u003eb:
Harmonize author names - Unifies author name variations across your library.
Examples of harmonization:
“J.R.R. Tolkien”, “JRR Tolkien”, “Tolkien, J.R.R.” → “J.R.R. Tolkien” “Asimov, Isaac”, “Isaac Asimov” → “Isaac Asimov” books:authors-harmonize # Preview books:authors-harmonize --apply # Apply books:authors-harmonize --exclude="J.R.R. Tolkien,Stephen King" # Skip specific authors Options:
--apply / -a: Apply changes (preview mode by default) --exclude: Comma-separated list of author names to skip
data.bookscheck#\u003ccode\u003ebooks:check\u: Check the integrity of all books.
data.booksextract-cover#\u003ccode\u003ebooks: Tries to extract the cover from all books that do not have one and saves it in the public/covers folder.
data.booksrelocate#\u003ccode\u003ebooks:relo: Relocate all books to their calculated folder. This is necessary only if you want Biblioteca to manage your library structure.
data.booksscan#\u003ccode\u003ebooks:scan\u00: Will scan the public/books folder and add all books to the database. If a book already exists, it will be updated.
data.booksseries-harmonize#\u003ccode\u003ebo:
Detect series and clean titles - Identifies book series, assigns series index, and cleans up redundant information from titles.
Examples:
“Dune 2 Dune Messiah” → title: “Dune Messiah”, series: “Dune”, index: 2 “Foundation 1” → title: “Foundation”, series: “Foundation”, index: 1 books:series-harmonize # Preview books:series-harmonize --apply # Apply books:series-harmonize --exclude=84,142,143 # Skip specific book IDs Options:
--apply / -a: Apply changes (preview mode by default) --exclude: Comma-separated list of book IDs to skip Series names are kept in their original language (e.g., “Foundation” stays “Foundation”, not translated).
data.bookstags-harmonize#\u003ccode\u003ebook:
Harmonize tags and assign genres - Assigns 1-2 main genres from a predefined list, curates existing tags (keeps meaningful ones, removes junk), and may add descriptive tags.
The hybrid approach:
Assigns 1-2 main genres from a curated list of 37 literary genres Preserves meaningful existing tags (themes, settings, mood, etc.) Removes junk tags (“Book”, “Ebook”, “General”, “Fiction”, etc.) May add 1-2 new descriptive tags when clearly applicable books:tags-harmonize --language fr # Preview books:tags-harmonize --language fr --apply # Apply (replaces existing tags) books:tags-harmonize --language fr --mode add --apply # Add genres to existing tags books:tags-harmonize --language fr --exclude=42,99 # Skip specific book IDs Options:
--apply / -a: Apply changes (preview mode by default) --language / -l: Target language for genres (fr, en, de, es). Default: en --mode / -m: replace (default) replaces all tags, add keeps existing tags --exclude: Comma-separated list of book IDs to skip

data: A few users have been using Biblioteca for a while now without any major issue. However, as with any tool, there might be bugs or issues. If you find any, please report them and always make backups of your data and files. On every book page, on the bottom of the page, there is a button to delete the book. This will remove the book from the database and the filesystem.
There are different ways to edit the metadata of a book in Biblioteca.
data.multiple-book-edition-at-once#Multiple b: On the top right of the list of books, you can change the mode to list mode instead of the gallery view. On top of the list of books, you can find a button to edit all displayed books at once for serie, author and tags.
data.on-the-all-books-page#On the \u0026ldquo: On the top right of the list of books, you can change the mode to list mode instead of the gallery view. In list mode, you can edit the title, author and serie of a book.
data.on-the-book-page#On the book page:
On the book page, for each editable field you can click on the pencil icon to make the field editable.
After you have made the changes, you can click on the checkmark icon to save the changes.

data: You can manage the file structure of your book by hand and run a command to have them in biblioteca, or you can ask biblioteca to relocate them to a path format of your choice.
data.available-placeholders#Available placeho: {author} => Author, lowercase, slugified, {author-uc} => Author, first letters capitalized, {author-raw} => Author raw, {authorFirst} => First letter of author's name, {title} => Title, lowercase, slugified, {title-uc} => Title, first letters capitalized {title-raw} => Title raw {serie} => serie, lowercase, slugified {serie-uc} => Serie, first letters capitalized {serie-raw} => Serie raw {serieIndex} => Index in serie {language} => Language of the book, {extension} => Book extension,

data: Go to /configuration to configure or see the .env.local variables that can be configured.

Biblioteca supports remote authentication via HTTP headers, allowing you to integrate with authentication proxies like Authentik, Keycloak, or Pocket-ID. This feature enables single sign-on (SSO) capabilities where authentication is handled by an external service.
data.1-authentication-flow#1. Authentication:
The typical flow is:
User accesses Biblioteca Reverse proxy intercepts the request If not authenticated, proxy redirects to authentication service After authentication, proxy forwards request to Biblioteca with authentication header Biblioteca reads the header and logs in the user
data.1-configure-keycloak-gatekeeper#1. Confi: Keycloak uses a Gatekeeper (or newer Keycloak Gatekeeper) to handle authentication as a reverse proxy.
data.1-configure-outpost#1. Configure Outpost:
In Authentik, you’ll need to set up an Outpost (Proxy Provider) that will handle authentication and forward requests to Biblioteca.
Go to Applications → Outposts in Authentik Create a new Outpost or edit an existing one Configure the Forward auth settings: Set the External Host to your Biblioteca URL Enable Forward auth (single application)
data.1-configure-pocket-id#1. Configure Pocke: Set up Pocket-ID as a reverse proxy in front of Biblioteca Configure Pocket-ID to authenticate users Set the header that Pocket-ID will use to pass the authenticated username
data.2-configure-header-mapping#2. Configure:
In your Outpost configuration, set up header mapping to pass the authenticated user:
In the Outpost settings, go to Header Mapping Add a header mapping: Header Name: X-Remote-User (or your preferred header name) Expression: user.username or user.email (depending on what identifier you use in Biblioteca) Alternatively, you can use Authentik’s default headers:
X-Remote-User - contains the username X-Remote-Groups - contains user groups (if needed)
data.2-header-configuration#2. Header Configu: Pocket-ID typically uses the X-Remote-User header by default, but this can be configured in your Pocket-ID settings.
data.2-required-headers#2. Required Headers:
Your authentication proxy must set an HTTP header containing the authenticated username. Common header names include:
X-Remote-User X-Auth-Username X-Token-Subject Remote-User
data.2-using-keycloak-gatekeeper#2. Using Key:
Deploy Keycloak Gatekeeper in front of Biblioteca Configure it to authenticate users via Keycloak Set up header forwarding to pass the authenticated username Example configuration for Keycloak Gatekeeper:
listen: 0.0.0.0:3000 upstream-url: http://biblioteca:80 enable-json-logging: true enable-request-logging: true preserve-host: true forwarding-username: X-Remote-User
data.3-nginx-example#3. Nginx Example:
Here’s a general Nginx configuration that can work with various authentication proxies:
server { listen 80; server_name biblioteca.example.com; location / { # Forward to authentication proxy proxy_pass http://auth-proxy:8080; # Preserve original headers proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; # Forward authentication header to Biblioteca proxy_set_header X-Remote-User $http_x_remote_user; # If your proxy uses a different header, adjust accordingly # proxy_set_header X-Remote-User $http_x_auth_username; } }
data.4-example-nginx-configuration#4. Example:
If using Nginx with Pocket-ID:
location / { proxy_pass http://pocket-id:8080; proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; # Forward Pocket-ID authentication header proxy_set_header X-Remote-User $http_x_remote_user; }
data.4-reverse-proxy-configuration#4. Reverse:
If you’re using Nginx as a reverse proxy in front of Authentik:
location / { proxy_pass http://authentik-outpost:9000; proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; # Forward Authentik headers to Biblioteca proxy_set_header X-Remote-User $http_x_remote_user; }
data.4-using-nginx-with-keycloak#4. Using Ngi:
If using Nginx with Keycloak’s authentication module:
location / { auth_request /auth; auth_request_set $user $upstream_http_x_remote_user; proxy_set_header X-Remote-User $user; proxy_pass http://biblioteca:80; } location = /auth { internal; proxy_pass https://keycloak.example.com/auth/realms/your-realm/protocol/openid-connect/userinfo; proxy_pass_request_body off; proxy_set_header Content-Length ""; proxy_set_header X-Original-URI $request_uri; }
data.authentik#Authentik: Authentik is an open-source identity provider that can act as a reverse proxy for authentication.
data.configuration#Configuration:
To enable remote authentication, set the REMOTE_USER_LOGIN_HEADER_NAME environment variable in your .env.local file:
REMOTE_USER_LOGIN_HEADER_NAME=HTTP_X_TOKEN_SUBJECT The header name should be in the format HTTP_ where is the actual HTTP header name in uppercase with hyphens replaced by underscores. For example:
Header X-Token-Subject becomes HTTP_X_TOKEN_SUBJECT Header X-Remote-User becomes HTTP_X_REMOTE_USER Header X-Auth-Username becomes HTTP_X_AUTH_USERNAME You can find the exact header name format by checking your proxy’s documentation or inspecting the HTTP headers in your browser’s developer tools.
data.disabling-remote-authentication#Disablin:
To disable remote authentication, simply remove or comment out the REMOTE_USER_LOGIN_HEADER_NAME environment variable, or set it to an empty string:
REMOTE_USER_LOGIN_HEADER_NAME= When disabled, Biblioteca will fall back to its standard form-based authentication.
data.general-reverse-proxy-setup#General Reve: If you’re using a different authentication proxy or setting up a custom solution, here’s a general guide:
data.how-it-works#How It Works:
When remote authentication is enabled, Biblioteca reads the authenticated username from a configured HTTP header. The authentication proxy (Authentik, Keycloak, etc.) handles the login process and sets this header with the authenticated user’s identifier. Biblioteca then automatically logs in the user if they exist in the database.
The user must already exist in Biblioteca’s database. Remote authentication does not create users automatically. You must create users manually or through another mechanism before they can authenticate via remote authentication.
data.keycloak#Keycloak: Keycloak is another popular open-source identity and access management solution.
data.pocket-id#Pocket-ID: Pocket-ID is a lightweight authentication proxy solution.
data.testing-configuration#Testing Configurat:
You can test the header configuration by manually setting the header in a request:
curl -H "X-Remote-User: testuser" http://biblioteca.example.com/ Replace X-Remote-User with your configured header name (without the HTTP_ prefix).
data.user-not-logging-in#User Not Logging In:
Check Header Name: Verify that the header name in REMOTE_USER_LOGIN_HEADER_NAME matches what your proxy is sending. Remember the HTTP_ prefix and underscore format.
Check User Exists: Ensure the user exists in Biblioteca’s database with a username that matches the value in the authentication header.
Check Proxy Headers: Use browser developer tools or proxy logs to verify that the authentication header is being sent correctly.
Check Trusted Proxies: Verify that your proxy’s IP address is in the TRUSTED_PROXIES list.

data:
You can provide typesense with an embedding model that will allow it to suggest similar books based on your tags and on the book description.
To do so, add the following line to your .env:
TYPESENSE_EMBED_MODEL='ts/e5-small' This will use typesense’s integrated models to generate the embeddings and the vector search.
You can use your own ollama or online provider as well for embedding generation:
TYPESENSE_EMBED_MODEL='openai/nomic-embed-text' TYPESENSE_EMBED_URL='http://127.0.0.1:11434' TYPESENSE_EMBED_KEY='your key' TYPESENSE_EMBED_NUM_DIM=768 To disable this feature, for example in low memory environment, just provide an empty model:
TYPESENSE_EMBED_MODEL=''

The verification status is a flag on the books. When you add a book, it will be set to false.
If you are an administrator, there is a link in the application menu listing all boks that have not been verified yet.
This feature allows you to know which books have been added recently and for whom you need to verify and update the metadata.
When the flag is checked, the edition controls are removed until you uncheck it.

Biblioteca is configured to be easily themeable.
By default all the template in the templates directory are used unless the same template exists in the theme’s directory.
data.add-your-theme-in-the-user-form#Add your:
Edit src/Form/ProfileType.php:
src Form ProfileType.php ->add('theme', ChoiceType::class, [ 'label' => 'Theme', 'choices' => [ 'Default' => 'default', 'Dark' => 'dark', 'Cool Theme' => 'cool', ], ])
data.override-templates#Override templates:
You can overwrite all the original templates by adding a file with the same structure under your theme directory:
In templates/themes//[original_folder]/[original_template_name] you can rewrite the template with your theme markup
Every time a template is called, it will first check if the template exists in the theme’s folder or fall back to the original one
templates shelf index.html.twig themes cool shelf index.html.twig
data.test-the-theme#Test the theme: Don’t forget to change your theme in the user settings to test it.

data:
Biblioteca is currently available in French and English.
You can easily add new locales by following these steps:
data.add-the-language-to-the-forms#Add the la:
Edit UserType.php and ProfileType.php:
src Form UserType.php ProfileType.php ->add('language', ChoiceType::class, [ 'choices' => [ 'English' => 'en', 'French' => 'fr', 'name' => 'iso_code', ], ])
data.create-translation-files#Create translat:
Create new files for each category in the translations folder:
translations AutocompleteBundle.en.yaml AutocompleteBundle.iso_code.yaml KnpPaginatorBundle.en.yaml KnpPaginatorBundle.iso_code.yaml messages+intl-icu.en.yaml messages+intl-icu.iso_code.yaml
data.translate-the-files#Translate the files:
Open the files and translate them.
If you see missing translations or untranslated strings in the app, please report them so we can add them to the default translations.

data:
On your homepage, you will see the list of books that you have started reading and their progress.
You can also see the books that you have marked as “In my reading List” and below, some suggestions based on tags.
All the books series that you have started reading will also appear on your homepage, so you can see where you are in your progress. Completionists beware!
When you read books in the app or on your kobo, the progress will be updated in the app.

You can mark any book as “Read”, “Don’t want to read” or “In my reading List” by using the buttons below the book cover on the book’s page.
Books that you have marked as “Read” will appear in your reading timeline and bey greyed out in the lists. You can access your reading timeline by clicking on the “Reading timeline” link in the navigation bar.
Books that you have marked as “Don’t want to read” will be hidden from the lists and will not appear in your reading timeline.
Books that you have marked as “In my reading List” will appear on your homepage.
It is possible to sync your Kobo devices with Biblioteca.
data.copy-the-url#Copy the URL: An URL for your opds feed will be generated.
data.generate-a-token#Generate a token: Go to the “My Profile” page on the OPDS tab and click on the “Create token” button.

data: There are several ways to search for books in the library.
data.filtering#Filtering:
In the search bar, you can click on advanced filters, which will allow you to write a typesense filter query. The documentation for filtering typesense queries can be found here: https://typesense.org/docs/27.1/api/search.html#filter-parameters
If AI features are enabled, you can also write a natural language query and ask AI to convert it to filters by clicking the magic wand button:
data.quick-search#Quick search:
On top of every page, you can find a search bar. You can search for books by title, author, series, or tags. The search is case-insensitive and will return all books that match the search query.
This is mostly useful when you know what you are looking for and want to find it quickly.

data: Biblioteca allows you to send ebooks directly to your e-reader devices (such as Kindle, Kobo, or other devices that support email delivery) via email. This feature is similar to what Calibre-Web offers and makes it easy to transfer books to your devices.
data.book-doesnt-appear-on-my-device#Book doe: Check your e-reader’s email inbox or document delivery settings Verify that the email address you configured matches your device’s email address Some devices may require you to approve the sender email address in their settings Check your device’s documentation for any additional setup requirements
data.email-sending-fails#Email sending fails: Verify that your administrator has configured the MAILER_DSN setting correctly Check that the email address you configured is correct for your device Ensure the book file exists and is accessible Check the file size - it may exceed the maximum allowed size
data.file-size-limitations#File Size Limitati:
Email providers typically have file size limits for attachments (usually around 25 MB). If a book file exceeds this limit, you will see an error message and the book will not be sent. In this case, you may need to:
Use a different delivery method (such as downloading the book and transferring it manually) Contact your administrator to increase the file size limit if supported
data.finding-your-e-reader-email-address#Find:
Different e-reader manufacturers provide email addresses for sending books:
Kindle: Check your Amazon account settings under “Content and Devices” → “Preferences” → “Personal Document Settings”. Your Kindle will have an email address like yourname@kindle.com Kobo: Kobo devices don’t typically support email delivery, but you can use the Kobo sync feature instead Other devices: Check your device’s documentation or settings for the email address used for receiving documents
data.managing-your-e-reader-email-addresses#M:
You can manage your e-reader email addresses from your profile:
Edit: Click the “Edit” button next to any e-reader email to modify its name or email address Delete: Click the “Delete” button to remove an e-reader email address you no longer use Remember that you need at least one configured e-reader email address to use the send feature.
data.no-buttons-appear-on-the-book-page#No bu: Make sure you have added at least one e-reader email address in your profile settings Check that you are logged in to your account
data.prerequisites#Prerequisites:
Before you can use this feature, your administrator must configure the email settings. The feature requires:
A configured MAILER_DSN in the .env.local file (see Instance Configuration for administrators) Email sending capabilities enabled on the server
data.sending-books-to-your-e-reader#Sending B:
Once you have configured at least one e-reader email address, you can send books directly from the book detail page:
Navigate to any book in your library On the book detail page, you will see buttons for each of your configured e-reader devices (e.g., “Send to My Kindle”, “Send to Kobo Clara”) Click the button for the device you want to send the book to The book will be sent via email to that device’s email address You will see a confirmation message when the book is successfully sent The book will arrive in your e-reader’s email inbox, and most devices will automatically process and add it to your library.
data.setting-up-your-e-reader-email-addresses: Go to your profile by clicking on your username in the sidebar, then select “My Profile” Click on the “E-reader Emails” tab Click “Add E-reader Email” to add a new e-reader email address Fill in the form: Name: A friendly name for your device (e.g., “My Kindle”, “Kobo Clara”) Email: The email address associated with your e-reader device Click “Save” You can add multiple e-reader email addresses if you have multiple devices. Each device will appear as a separate option when sending books.

/biblioteca/guides/user/send-to-ereader/.title: Sending Books to E-readers via Email

data:
Shelves are groups of books, they can be static or dynamic.
You can manually add books to static shelves and that’s it.
You can also create dynamic shelves, which are based on a query. For example, you can create a shelf that contains all books from your favorite authors that you haven’t read and if you or an administrator adds books from these authors, they will automatically be listed in there.
data.create-a-dynamic-shelf#Create a dynamic: To do this, head to the “All books” page, and update the filters in the top Filters sections. When you are satisfied with the result, click Save current filter, input the name of your shelf and you’re good to go!
data.create-a-static-shelf#Create a static sh: In your side menu click on “Edit Shelves”. Here you can edit or delete all your existing shelves. At the bottom of the page, you can create a new Shelf. Once you have created a shelf, head on to the books you want to add in that shelves and below the cover, the list of the shelves will be displayed. Add them by clicking on the shelf’s name and remove them by clicking again.

data: It is possible to sync your Kobo devices with Biblioteca.
data.create-a-new-kobo-device#Create a new Ko: Go to the “My Profile” page on the Kobo Settings tab and click on the “Create new Kobo device” button.
data.create-a-static-shelf#Create a static sh: Create a new static shelf on your profile. And add some books in it.
data.follow-the-configuration-instructions#Fo:
Follow the instructions on the kobo device page in biblioteca.
You will need to plug your kobo device to your computer and edit a configuration file. The lines to edit and the values to enter are detailed in the app.
data.read-and-sync-again#Read and sync again: Read your books on the Kobo device and sync it again to update your progress on Biblioteca.
data.sync-the-kobo-device#Sync the Kobo devic: Once the configuration file is edited, unmount your kobo device and click on the “Sync” button on the kobo device.

data:
On the sidebar, you will see a panel with your username. Here you can click on the “My Profile link” to edit your profile.
On this page, you can change your password, the display language and enable or disable some menu categories based on your preferences.
You can also change your theme.
In the profile settings, you can also:
Configure Kobo device sync Set up e-reader email addresses for sending books via email Manage OPDS access If you are an administrator, you can also configure your AI settings

A few users have been using Biblioteca for a while now without any major issue. However, as with any tool, there might be bugs or issues. If you find any, please report them and always make backups of your data and files.
data.access-the-application#Access the applic:
Access the application at the URL given to you by the helm install command.
Log in with the name and password you set in the previous step.
values.ymlbiblioteca: appSecret: appSecret: zafmqUbgaMQbx4wCFbZSpwsQ34Dw7wUd persistence: enabled: true Check the Helm Chart for more information on the configuration options.
We recommend you set at least the appSecret values and decide if you want to persist any data.
data.finalize-installation#Finalize installat: Once installed, the Helm chart will output the instructions to finalize the app’s installation and the creation of your first admin user.
data.install-the-helm-chart#Install the Helm: helm repo add biblioverse https://biblioverse.github.io/helm/ helm install biblioteca biblioverse/biblioteca -f values.yml

data: A few users have been using Biblioteca for a while now without any major issue. However, as with any tool, there might be bugs or issues. If you find any, please report them and always make backups of your data and files.
data.access-the-application#Access the applic: Open http://localhost:8080 and log in with the name and password you set in the previous step.
data.create-a-docker-composeyml-file#Create a: docker-compose.ymlservices: biblioteca: image: ghcr.io/biblioverse/biblioteca:main command: ["/bin/sh", "-c" , "apache2-foreground" ] ports: - 8080 depends_on: - db stdin_open: true tty: true volumes: - :/var/www/html/public/covers - :/var/www/html/public/books - :/var/www/html/public/media - .env:/var/www/html/.env db: image: mariadb:12.2 environment: - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=db - MYSQL_DATABASE=db - MYSQL_USER=db - MYSQL_PASSWORD=db volumes: - mariadb:/var/lib/mysql typesense: image: typesense/typesense:29.0 restart: on-failure ports: - 8983 - 8108 volumes: - searchdata:/data command: '--data-dir /data --api-key=xyz --enable-cors' volumes: mariadb: searchdata: Don’t forget to replace the database password and the typesense. Change the folders with the path you want to use to store the books on your machine.
data.create-the-database-and-admin-user#Creat: docker compose exec biblioteca bin/console doctrine:migration:migrate --no-interaction docker compose exec biblioteca bin/console app:create-admin-user [name] [password] docker compose exec biblioteca bin/console biblioverse:typesense:populate

data: As a developer, you can use the script ./update.sh to update the application. If you have local changes in the application code, they will be overwritten by the latest release.
data.update-the-database-schema#Update the da: docker compose exec biblioteca bin/console doctrine:migration:migrate --no-interaction

data:
The following properties can be set in a .env.local file in the root of the project:
APP_ENV=prod APP_SECRET=9653a6c476d291323d2db7417c13a814 DATABASE_URL="mysql://db:db@db:3306/db?serverVersion=mariadb-10.3.39&charset=utf8" MESSENGER_TRANSPORT_DSN=doctrine://default?auto_setup=0 MAILER_DSN=native://default TYPESENSE_URL=http://typesense:8108 TYPESENSE_KEY=xyz BOOK_FOLDER_NAMING_FORMAT="{authorFirst}/{author}/{title}/{serie}" BOOK_FILE_NAMING_FORMAT="{serie}-{serieIndex}-{title}" KOBO_PROXY_USE_EVERYWHERE=0 KOBO_PROXY_ENABLED=1 ALLOW_BOOK_RELOCATION=1 EPUB_METADATA_EMBED_ENABLED=1 MAILER_DSN=smtp://user:pass@smtp.example.com:587 SMTP_FROM_EMAIL=noreply@example.com SMTP_FROM_NAME=Biblioteca SMTP_MAX_FILE_SIZE=25 APP_ENV: The environment the application is running in. This can be dev or prod. You should always use prod unless you need to debug the application. APP_SECRET: A secret key used to secure the application. Make it unique! DATABASE_URL: The URL to the database. You should not need to change this unless you are using a different database. MESSENGER_TRANSPORT_DSN: Do not change it. MAILER_DSN: Mailer DSN for sending ebooks to e-readers via email. See Sending Books to E-readers for user documentation. TYPESENSE_URL: The URL to the typesense server. You should not need to change this unless you are using a different server. TYPESENSE_KEY: The key to access the typesense server. Needs to correspond to the one you set in your docker-compose file. TYPESENSE_EMBED_MODEL: Embed model, default is the built-in ts/all-MiniLM-L12-v2 model. You can use an OpenAi/Palm/Vertex model. Example: openai/text-embedding-3-small. TYPESENSE_EMBED_NUM_DIM: Dimension of the embed model. all-MiniLM-L12-v2 is 384, text-embedding-3-small is 1536. Read your model documentation to know the dimension. TYPESENSE_EMBED_KEY: Authentication for the embed model. Default is ~, but you can set a token if you use an external model. BOOK_FOLDER_NAMING_FORMAT: The format to use to name the folders where the books are stored. You can use the following placeholders: {authorFirst}, {author}, {title}, {serie}. MAILER_DSN: Mailer DSN for sending ebooks to e-readers. This uses Symfony’s Mailer component. Examples: SMTP: smtp://user:pass@smtp.example.com:587 SMTP with TLS: smtp://user:pass@smtp.example.com:587?encryption=tls SMTP with SSL: smtp://user:pass@smtp.example.com:465?encryption=ssl For cloud services, you can use provider-specific DSNs (see Symfony Mailer documentation) SMTP_FROM_EMAIL: Default “from” email address for sent emails. SMTP_FROM_NAME: Default “from” name for sent emails (default: Biblioteca). SMTP_MAX_FILE_SIZE: Maximum file size in MB for email attachments (default: 25). BOOK_FILE_NAMING_FORMAT: The format to use to name the files where the books are stored. You can use the following placeholders: {serie}, {serieIndex}, {title}. KOBO_PROXY_USE_DEV: If set to 1, the kobo proxy will be used in development. KOBO_PROXY_USE_EVERYWHERE: If set to 1, the kobo proxy will be used everywhere and all request will be forwarded to the original store. KOBO_PROXY_ENABLED: If set to 0, the kobo proxy will be disabled. ALLOW_BOOK_RELOCATION: If set to 0, the books will not be moved to the correct folder when added to the library. This is useful if you want to manage the folder structure yourself. REMOTE_USER_LOGIN_HEADER_NAME: You can handle authentication from your proxy and read the HTTP Header to authenticate the user. Default value is empty string, Example: HTTP_X_TOKEN_SUBJECT. User must exist on the database. See Remote Authentication for detailed setup instructions for Authentik, Keycloak, Pocket-ID, and other authentication proxies. EPUB_METADATA_EMBED_ENABLED: If set to 0, EPUB downloads will use the original file without embedding library metadata.
You can use and install Biblioteca on your Unraid system by using a docker-compose manager, like Dockge
This documentation is hosted on GitHub and is open source. If you find an error or would like to contribute, please feel free to make a pull request or open an issue on the project.

Please feel free to open issues or start discussions on GitHub if you have any questions or need help. We are happy to help you.
New features and merge requests are also welcome!
